title: Leader-Follower Rivalry Expert for Undercut and Cover-Stop Behavior
summary: Recent OR work shows that accounting for competition materially changes optimal pit timing, and official F1 strategy explainers emphasize undercut/overcut and cover-stop logic.
rationale: Recent OR work shows that accounting for competition materially changes optimal pit timing, and official F1 strategy explainers emphasize undercut/overcut and cover-stop logic. Your current best features encode self state well, but they do not explicitly model leader-versus-follower pressure within a race-lap.
implementation_hint: Build race-lap relative features such as local percentile of lap-time delta, tyre-life rank within race-lap, vulnerable-to-undercut flags, and close-rival pressure by position bands. Train a specialist only on rows where a nearby rival context exists, then blend with the global model.
expected_effect: Should help rows near real strategic interactions, especially mid-race windows where many stops are responses rather than absolute tyre-life thresholds.
risk: The dataset lacks explicit time gaps, so interaction must be proxied from rank/position/lap-time signals; weak proxies may add noise.
